commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=584a3432be5fb728ffa183568db1cc2101993076)
commands.review_summary:
The branch makes Codex account display prefer explicit auth order over `lastGood`, threads `contextEngineSessionKey` through heartbeat/embedded/Codex context-engine paths, and adds regression coverage.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current main's Codex account display checks `lastGood` before ordered candidates when no live account profile is available, matching the relogin/order scenario in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/84386. I did not run a live OAuth relogin in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: A maintainer should decide whether to close in favor of the narrower linked PR or ask for a split branch; automation cannot supply the missing live proof or make the session-state scope decision.

target.title: Fix stale Codex lastGood after explicit auth order relogin
target.body:
# Summary
- Make Codex account overview treat explicit `openai-codex` auth order as authoritative when choosing the active profile, so a fresh relogin profile no longer yields stale `lastGood`.
- Thread `contextEngineSessionKey` through Codex compaction, run-attempt, thread-lifecycle, and heartbeat paths so context-engine operations use the correct session identity.
- Add a regression test covering the relogin case from #84386.

# Verification
- Behavior addressed: stale `lastGood` no longer overrides explicit Codex order in the `/codex account` view.
- Real environment tested: local repo on macOS; no live OAuth round-trip.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `pnpm test extensions/codex/src/commands.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
  - `git diff --check`
  - `pnpm changed:lanes --json`
  - `pnpm check:changed`
- Evidence after fix: 89 tests passed in `extensions/codex/src/commands.test.ts`, including the new relogin regression.
- Observed result after fix: the fresh profile is shown as active and the stale default is no longer selected first.
- What was not tested: a live Codex OAuth relogin round trip.
- Known proof gap: `pnpm check:changed` reached unrelated `lint apps` work and failed because `swiftlint` is not installed in this environment.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: stale `lastGood` no longer overrides explicit Codex auth profile order in `/codex account` display. After a user relogins with a fresh profile and sets an explicit auth order, the account overview now correctly shows the explicitly-ordered fresh profile as active instead of the stale `lastGood` default.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on branch `codex/fix-84386-codex-auth-order-lastgood`, Node 24.14.1, macOS 26.4.1 arm64.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/commands.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/compact.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts`
2. `pnpm check:changed`
3. `git diff --check`

Evidence after fix (Vitest — 123 tests across 3 test files, including the relogin regression):
```
Test Files  3 passed (3)
     Tests  123 passed (123)
```

The relogin regression test `prefers explicit Codex auth order over stale lastGood after relogin` (commands.test.ts:1140) directly simulates the #84386 scenario:
- Two `openai-codex` profiles: `openai-codex:work` (fresh, explicitly ordered first) and `openai-codex:default` (stale, referenced by `lastGood`)
- Explicit auth order is `["openai-codex:work", "openai-codex:default"]`
- `lastGood` state still points to `openai-codex:default`
- The test asserts the fresh `work@example.test` profile is shown as active (`active now`), not the stale default

The account overview output correctly lists:
```
1. work@example.test   ChatGPT subscription   — active now
2. default@example.test   ChatGPT subscription   — available if needed
```

Observed result after fix:
- `resolveDisplayAuthOrder` now returns `{ order, explicit }` instead of just `order[]`, flagging when the user has configured an explicit auth order
- `resolveActiveProfileId` checks explicit order candidates BEFORE falling back to stale `lastGood` state
- The fresh explicitly-ordered profile is correctly selected as active
- Context-engine session key threading ensures isolated heartbeat/compaction context-engine state is keyed correctly per run

What was not tested: live Codex OAuth relogin round trip (requires active subscription-backed Codex account with two valid OAuth profiles).
target.author: nianjiuzst
